message:
feat(e2e/mt-mvcc-bench): p50/p95/p99 reporting over iterations

mt_mvcc_bench previously reported only the best (min wall-clock)
across --iters samples, which hid tail variance exactly when MVCC
contention showed up worst. Reworked the reporting layer to compute
proper percentiles while still leaving the single-run shape intact.

New types and helpers:
  - RunResult::elapsed_ms() — convenience accessor alongside the
    existing writes_per_sec().
  - RunStats { samples: Vec<RunResult> } — holds the collected
    samples from one (threads, backend) configuration.
  - RunStats::total_failed_rows, p50/p95/p99 for both
    writes_per_sec and elapsed_ms.
  - percentile_by(value_fn, p) — generic over which field to
    extract.
  - percentile_value(vec, p) — linear-interpolation percentile on
    a Vec<f64>, the one-stop helper that the typed wrappers call.
  - collect_samples(iters, f) — runs the closure `iters` times and
    wraps the Vec in a RunStats.

Output table now has:
  threads | fsqlite_wps | sqlite_wps | throughput_ratio | ms_p50 | ms_p95 | ms_p99 | ...

and documents a new time_ratio = fsqlite_batch_ms / sqlite_batch_ms
metric so a reader can see time-side contention separately from
throughput-side contention.

Doc comment at the top of the file updated to describe the new
shape. The --iters flag no longer means "report min of N"; it
means "collect N samples and report percentiles over them." Old
--iters=3 behavior is effectively the new --iters=3 with the
percentile narrative.
